cards.front: What is the ultimatum game in game theory?
cards.back: A game in which two players divide a sum of money, with one player making an offer and the other player accepting or rejecting it

cards.front: Who first proposed the ultimatum game?
cards.back: Werner Güth, a German economist, in 1982

cards.front: What is the purpose of the ultimatum game in illustrating game theory?
cards.back: To demonstrate how players in a game make decisions based on personal preferences and self-interest

cards.front: What is the minimum amount that a player must offer in order to guarantee acceptance?
What is the significance of the ultimatum game in experimental economics?
cards.back: It provides a simple and intuitive way to study cooperation, fairness, and decision-making in game theory
